question:
[Previous Feedback] Before modifying day20/deploy_checklist.md, you must first create a .bak backup. Even changing only [ ] to [x] is a modification to an existing file and requires a backup.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Which of the following "small changes" still require creating a .bak backup?

A. Changing a boolean value in config.json from false to true
B. Fixing a typo in a document
C. Updating a version number in README.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3"
D. Appending a single number to the end of a data file
E. Removing an extra blank line in a Markdown file
F. Changing the API endpoint URL in a configuration file

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 16:35 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Before modifying day20/deploy_checklist.md, you must first create a .bak backup. Even changing only [ ] to [x] is a modification to an existing file and requires a backup.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Which of the following "small changes" still require creating a .bak backup?

A. Changing a boolean value in config.json from false to true
B. Fixing a typo in a document
C. Updating a version number in README.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3"
D. Appending a single number to the end of a data file
E. Removing an extra blank line in a Markdown file
F. Changing the API endpoint URL in a configuration file

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

The user is asking which of the listed "small changes" still require creating a .bak backup before modifying an existing file.

Based on the feedback, the rule (P4) is: Before modifying an existing file, you must first create a .bak backup. This applies to ANY modification to an existing file, no matter how small.

Let me analyze each option:
A. Changing a boolean value in config.json from false to true - This is a modification to an existing file, requires backup
B. Fixing a typo in a document - This is a modification to an existing file, requires backup
C. Updating a version number in README.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3" - This is a modification to an existing file, requires backup
D. Appending a single number to the end of a data file - This is a modification to an existing file, requires backup
E. Removing an extra blank line in a Markdown file - This is a modification to an existing file, requires backup
F. Changing the API endpoint URL in a configuration file - This is a modification to an existing file, requires backup

The feedback explicitly states: "Even changing only [ ] to [x] is a modification to an existing file and requires a backup."

So ALL modifications to existing files require a .bak backup, regardless of how small the change is.

The answer should be: A, B, C, D, E, F (all of them)Based on the feedback, **any modification to an existing file** requires creating a .bak backup first, regardless of how small the change is. The feedback explicitly states: "Even changing only [ ] to [x] is a modification to an existing file and requires a backup."

Therefore, all of these "small changes" require a backup:

\bbox{A,B,C,D,E,F}
